welcome.title: Welcome to Le Divino, restaurant in Agde
welcome.description: In the heart of Agde city centre, on Place Jean Jaurès, Le Divino welcomes you in a warm and elegant setting. Discover traditional French and Mediterranean cuisine, crafted from fresh, seasonal products, elevated by our chef's expertise. Just 5 minutes from Cap d'Agde, our restaurant with its shaded terrace is the perfect spot for a memorable lunch or dinner.

cookies.title: Cookie Management Policy
cookies.seo_description: Cookie management policy for Le Divino restaurant. Information about Google Analytics 4, consent and settings.
cookies.intro_text: The website www.ledivino-agde.fr, published by Le Divino (SIRET: 99935890600017), uses cookies and trackers. This policy informs you about their nature, purpose and the means at your disposal to manage them, in accordance with the ePrivacy Directive and CNIL recommendations.
cookies.what_text: A cookie is a small text file stored on your device (computer, smartphone, tablet) when you visit a website. It allows the site to remember information about your visit (preferred language, pages viewed, etc.) in order to facilitate your subsequent browsing.
cookies.essential_text: These cookies are essential for the website to function and cannot be disabled. They do not store any personal data.
cookies.analytics_text: These cookies allow us to measure website traffic and analyse visitor behaviour in order to improve our services. They are only placed after your explicit consent.
cookies.analytics_provider: Provider: Google LLC — Data is transferred to the United States under the Data Privacy Framework (DPF).
cookies.consent_text: On your first visit, a consent banner allows you to:
cookies.consent_refuse: Refuse non-essential cookies (only strictly necessary cookies remain active)
cookies.consent_customize: Customise your preferences by enabling or disabling each category
cookies.consent_duration: Your choice is saved for a period of 13 months. You may change your preferences at any time by deleting your cookies through your browser settings.
cookies.browser_text: You can configure your browser to accept or refuse cookies. Below are links to the settings of the main browsers:
cookies.optout_text: You can also specifically disable Google Analytics by installing the browser add-on provided by Google, available for Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge and Opera.
cookies.update_text: This cookie policy may be amended at any time to reflect legal or technical developments. The date of the last update is indicated below.
cookies.contact_text: For any questions regarding cookies or the protection of your data, please contact us:

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.seo_description: Privacy policy and personal data protection for Le Divino restaurant, in compliance with the GDPR.
privacy.intro_text: The restaurant Le Divino (SIRET: 99935890600017), located at 5 place Jean Jaurès, 34300 Agde, France, is committed to protecting the privacy of users of its website www.ledivino-agde.fr.
privacy.intro_commitment: This privacy policy is intended to inform you about how we collect, use and protect your personal data, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR — EU Regulation 2016/679) and the French Data Protection Act (Loi Informatique et Libertés).
privacy.data_collected_intro: We collect the following data in connection with the use of our website:
privacy.data_reservation_desc: Last name, first name, email address, phone number, preferred date and time, number of guests, special requests (allergies, occasions).
privacy.data_contact_desc: Name, email address, subject and message content.
privacy.data_navigation_desc: Browsing data collected via Google Analytics 4 (anonymised IP address, pages viewed, visit duration, device type, browser). This data is only collected with your prior consent.
privacy.purposes_intro: Your data is processed for the following purposes:
privacy.purpose_analytics: Improving user experience and statistical analysis of the website (Google Analytics 4)
privacy.purpose_legal: Compliance with legal and regulatory obligations
privacy.legal_basis_interest_desc: (Article 6(1)(f)): for responding to contact requests and improving our services.
privacy.recipients_intro: Your personal data may be shared with the following recipients:
privacy.recipient_vercel: (website hosting) — United States, covered by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
privacy.recipient_google: (Google Analytics 4) — United States, covered by the Data Privacy Framework
privacy.recipients_no_sale: No data is sold or transferred to third parties for commercial purposes.
privacy.rights_title: 7. Your Rights (Articles 15 to 22 of the GDPR)
privacy.rights_intro: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacy.right_access_desc: (Art. 15): obtain confirmation that data concerning you is being processed and receive a copy.
privacy.right_rectification_desc: (Art. 16): have inaccurate or incomplete data corrected.
privacy.right_erasure_desc: (Art. 17): request the deletion of your data under the conditions provided by law.
privacy.right_restriction_desc: (Art. 18): request the restriction of processing of your data.
privacy.right_portability_desc: (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
privacy.right_objection_desc: (Art. 21): object to the processing of your data on legitimate grounds.
privacy.right_withdrawal_desc: (Art. 7): withdraw your consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal.
privacy.rights_contact: To exercise these rights, contact us at:
privacy.rights_response: We undertake to respond within 30 days. In the event of a dispute, you may lodge a complaint with the CNIL (French National Commission on Informatics and Liberty) — cnil.fr.
privacy.security_intro: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure or destruction:
privacy.cookies_text: For more information about cookies used on this website, please refer to our
privacy.update_text: This privacy policy may be amended at any time. The date of the last update is indicated below. We encourage you to review it regularly.

restaurant-agde.hero_subtitle: Traditional French and Mediterranean cuisine, on Place Jean Jaurès
restaurant-agde.intro_title: Le Divino, your restaurant in Agde
restaurant-agde.intro_text: In the heart of Agde's historic centre, Le Divino is more than just a restaurant: it's an invitation to discover the richness of traditional French cuisine. Located on Place Jean Jaurès, our establishment welcomes you in an elegant and friendly setting where every meal becomes a special moment.
restaurant-agde.cuisine_text: Every day, our chef selects the finest fresh products from local Languedoc producers. From starters to desserts, each dish tells a story: that of a generous terroir, elevated by traditional expertise. Meats, fish, seasonal vegetables — our menu evolves with the markets.
restaurant-agde.ambiance_title: A unique setting in the city centre
restaurant-agde.ambiance_text: On Place Jean Jaurès, at the foot of Saint-Étienne Cathedral, Le Divino enjoys an exceptional location. The warm, intimate interior contrasts with the large shaded terrace overlooking the square. Ideal for a sunny lunch or a candlelit dinner.
restaurant-agde.terrasse_text: Enjoy our spacious terrace on Place Jean Jaurès, in the shade of the plane trees.
restaurant-agde.produits_text: Daily sourcing from Languedoc producers and the Agde market halls.
restaurant-agde.service_text: A passionate team that takes care of every detail for your comfort.
restaurant-agde.parking_text: Free public car park just 2 minutes' walk from the restaurant.
restaurant-agde.horaires_text: Le Divino is open 7 days a week. Find us at 5 Place Jean Jaurès, 34300 Agde — just 5 minutes by car from Cap d'Agde.
restaurant-agde.cta_text: By phone or online, book your table at Le Divino.

restaurant-terrasse-agde.hero_subtitle: Outdoor lunch and dinner, Place Jean Jaurès
restaurant-terrasse-agde.intro_title: The finest terrace in Agde city centre
restaurant-terrasse-agde.intro_text: Looking for a restaurant with a terrace in Agde? Le Divino offers one of the most beautiful terraces in the historic centre. Set on Place Jean Jaurès, beneath century-old plane trees, our spacious terrace is the perfect spot to enjoy quality French cuisine outdoors.
restaurant-terrasse-agde.terrasse_text: Our shaded terrace seats over 40 guests in an authentic Mediterranean setting. Facing Place Jean Jaurès and near Saint-Étienne Cathedral, you'll enjoy a unique atmosphere blending historic heritage and southern joie de vivre.
restaurant-terrasse-agde.dejeuner_text: At lunchtime, our sunny terrace is perfect for a gourmet break. Market menu from €24.90 with starter, main course and dessert — all prepared with fresh daily products. A real moment of relaxation in the heart of Agde.
restaurant-terrasse-agde.diner_text: In the evening, the terrace transforms. Soft lighting, candles on the tables, refined cuisine: the atmosphere is romantic and festive. Perfect for a dinner for two or with friends on beautiful summer evenings.
restaurant-terrasse-agde.saisons_text: Our terrace is set up to welcome you in spring and summer alike. In winter, our interior dining room offers the same level of comfort in a warm, cosy atmosphere.
restaurant-terrasse-agde.avantage_1: Terrace shaded by the square's plane trees
restaurant-terrasse-agde.avantage_2: View of Place Jean Jaurès and the historic centre
restaurant-terrasse-agde.avantage_3: Outdoor service from lunch to dinner
restaurant-terrasse-agde.cta_text: Secure your terrace spot by booking in advance.

restaurant-cap-agde.hero_subtitle: Le Divino — just 5 minutes away, in Agde city centre
restaurant-cap-agde.intro_title: The authentic alternative to Cap d'Agde
restaurant-cap-agde.intro_text: Staying in Cap d'Agde and looking for a quality restaurant? Just 5 minutes by car, Le Divino offers an authentic culinary experience away from the tourist hustle. Traditional French cuisine, fresh products, shaded terrace — discover the true taste of Languedoc.
restaurant-cap-agde.pourquoi_title: Why leave the Cap to dine in Agde?
restaurant-cap-agde.pourquoi_text: Cap d'Agde is full of tourist restaurants, but regulars know: the best tables are in Agde city centre. At Le Divino, no standard menus — our chef creates his menu based on daily market arrivals, with locally sourced products selected each morning.
restaurant-cap-agde.acces_text: From Cap d'Agde, take the D32E4 towards Agde centre. In 5 minutes, you're on Place Jean Jaurès. Free public car park 2 minutes' walk from the restaurant. A short journey for an incomparable culinary experience.
restaurant-cap-agde.trajet_text: Via the D32E4, towards Agde centre from the Cap.
restaurant-cap-agde.parking_text: Public car park 2 min walk away, unlike the Cap where parking is difficult.
restaurant-cap-agde.terrasse_text: Dine on the terrace on Place Jean Jaurès, in the calm of the historic centre.
restaurant-cap-agde.prix_text: Reasonable prices for quality cuisine, far from the tourist mark-ups of the Cap.
restaurant-cap-agde.carte_text: Traditional French and Mediterranean cuisine: meats, fish, Languedoc specialities. Market menu from €24.90. Carefully selected regional wine list.
restaurant-cap-agde.avis_title: What our Cap d'Agde guests say
restaurant-cap-agde.avis_text: Our guests from Cap d'Agde keep coming back. They appreciate the quality of the cuisine, the authentic setting and the warm welcome — a true discovery just minutes from the seaside resort.
restaurant-cap-agde.cta_text: Book your table at Le Divino and enjoy a memorable dinner 5 min from the Cap.

promenade.seo_title: Restaurant near the Agde Riverside Walk | Le Divino — French Cuisine
promenade.seo_description: Discover Le Divino, a restaurant just steps from the Agde riverside walk. Traditional French cuisine, terrace on Place Jean Jaurès. Perfect after a stroll.
promenade.seo_keywords: restaurant Agde riverside, dining Agde old town, terrace restaurant Agde, lunch Agde historic centre
promenade.og_title: Restaurant near the Agde Riverside Walk | Le Divino
promenade.og_description: Traditional French cuisine a stone's throw from the Agde riverside walk. Terrace, local produce, warm atmosphere on Place Jean Jaurès.
promenade.og_image_alt: Terrace of Le Divino restaurant near the Agde riverside walk
promenade.hero_alt: Le Divino restaurant near the Agde riverside walk, terrace on Place Jean Jaurès
promenade.h1: Restaurant near the Agde Riverside Walk
promenade.hero_subtitle: A gourmet break just steps from the banks of the Hérault
promenade.s1_title: The Agde Riverside Walk: a heritage jewel along the Hérault
promenade.s1_p1: The Agde riverside walk is one of those places where time seems to slow down. Following the banks of the Hérault, it offers a unique view over the old town, its black basalt stone façades and ochre-tiled roofs standing out against the southern sky. Whether you are passing through or a long-time resident, this riverside stroll remains a must for anyone wishing to grasp the soul of Agde.
promenade.s1_p2: The quays, lined with century-old plane trees, welcome walkers and cyclists at all hours. In the morning, soft light caresses the city's volcanic stones. In late afternoon, the golden reflections of the setting sun on the waters of the Hérault compose a scene one never tires of. It is here, between the river and the lanes of the historic centre, that the true heart of the town beats, far from the summer bustle of Cap d'Agde.
promenade.s1_p3: The path winds from the downstream bridge to the surroundings of Saint-Étienne Cathedral, passing restored fishermen's houses, secret gardens and a few café terraces where one gladly lingers. Each season brings its own charm: budding plane trees in spring, their generous shade in summer, the tawny hues of autumn and the crystalline calm of winter. The Agde riverside walk is far more than a simple path — it is an invitation to wander, a link between historic heritage and the Mediterranean art of living.
promenade.s2_image_alt: View over the Hérault from the centre of Agde, near Le Divino restaurant
promenade.s2_p1: Founded more than 2,600 years ago by the Greeks of Phocaea under the name Agatha Tyche, Agde is one of the oldest towns in France. The walk that follows the river bears witness to this age-old history. Strolling along the quays, you come across the vestiges of a glorious maritime past: former merchants' warehouses, wrought-iron mooring rings and stone stairways descending to the banks where fishing boats once moored.
promenade.s2_p2: Basalt stone, the dark volcanic material that earned Agde its nickname of "Black Pearl of the Mediterranean", is everywhere along the walk. It clads the walls of houses, paves certain stretches of the path and lends the whole a singular atmosphere, at once austere and profoundly elegant. It is an urban landscape found nowhere else along the Languedoc coast.
promenade.s2_p3: As you stroll, be sure to notice the architectural details: carved lintels, studded doors, mullioned windows. Every façade tells a chapter of Agde's history, from the Middle Ages to the Renaissance, from the Wars of Religion to the rise of contemporary tourism.
promenade.s3_title: Le Divino: an exceptional table just steps from the walk
promenade.s3_image_alt: Warm interior of Le Divino restaurant in Agde
promenade.s3_p1: At the end of your walk — or to punctuate it with a gourmet break — Le Divino welcomes you at 5 Place Jean Jaurès, just a few metres from the banks of the Hérault. Over the seasons, this traditional French restaurant has become a favourite spot for food lovers exploring the historic centre of Agde.
promenade.s3_p2: Set on one of the loveliest squares of the old town, Le Divino has a shaded terrace where it is a pleasure to sit after a long walk. The atmosphere is both relaxed and refined: carefully laid tables, soft lighting in the evening and attentive service that lets you savour every moment. Place Jean Jaurès, with its basalt façades and fountain, offers a dining setting long remembered.
promenade.s3_p3: Le Divino's cuisine showcases the produce of the Languedoc region and the catch of the nearby Mediterranean. The chef creates dishes where French tradition meets southern inspiration: fresh fish of the day, meats selected from local producers, seasonal vegetables from the Agde market. The wine list, meanwhile, gives pride of place to the appellations of Languedoc and the Rhône Valley, with a few gems from elsewhere for the curious palate.
promenade.s4_title: Why have lunch or dinner near the Agde riverside walk?
promenade.s4_card1_text: Place Jean Jaurès offers the feel of a Provençal village in the very heart of the historic town, far from the tourist bustle.
promenade.s4_card2_text: Settle in the open air to enjoy the Mediterranean mildness while savouring refined cuisine.
promenade.s4_card3_text: The chef favours short supply chains: fish from Grau d'Agde, vegetables from the market, Languedoc wines.
promenade.s4_card4_text: Just steps from the quays, Le Divino is the natural stop to extend the pleasure of a walk along the Hérault.
promenade.s5_title: A journey between heritage and gastronomy
promenade.s5_p1: To make the most of your visit to Agde, here is an itinerary we love to recommend. Begin with a morning walk along the Hérault, starting from Quai du Chapitre. Admire the view over the river and the roofs of the old town. Continue to <link>Saint-Étienne Cathedral</link>, an imposing basalt fortress that has watched over the town since the 12th century.
promenade.s5_p2: After your visit to the cathedral, walk back up the street to Place Jean Jaurès — that is where Le Divino awaits you for lunch on the terrace or in the restaurant's elegant dining room. At midday, the set menu of the day lets you discover the chef's cuisine at a gentle price, while in the evening the full menu invites a more elaborate meal, paired with the region's finest wines.
promenade.s5_p3: In the afternoon, extend your discovery with a visit to the <link>Agathois Museum</link>, a few minutes' walk away, to immerse yourself in the town's fascinating history and admire the famous Ephebe of Agde. A complete programme blending culture, heritage and the pleasures of the table — everything that makes a day in the historic centre of Agde so charming.
promenade.practical_hours: Tuesday to Saturday 12pm-2pm / 7pm-10pm — Sunday 12pm-3.30pm — Closed Monday
promenade.practical_access: A 2-minute walk from the riverside walk along the banks of the Hérault
promenade.cta_title: Book your table after the walk
promenade.cta_text: A stroll along the Hérault deserves a fine table. Book online or call us for lunch on the terrace or a candlelit dinner on Place Jean Jaurès.

cathedrale.seo_title: Restaurant next to Agde Cathedral | Le Divino — Place Jean Jaurès
cathedrale.seo_description: Le Divino, a restaurant next to Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde. Traditional French cuisine, terrace on Place Jean Jaurès. Lunch or dinner after your visit.
cathedrale.seo_keywords: restaurant Agde cathedral, restaurant Place Jean Jaurès Agde, dining near Saint-Étienne cathedral
cathedrale.og_title: Restaurant next to Agde Cathedral | Le Divino
cathedrale.og_description: Traditional French cuisine right next to Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde. Terrace, local produce, Place Jean Jaurès.
cathedrale.og_image_alt: Le Divino restaurant next to Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde
cathedrale.hero_alt: Le Divino restaurant next to Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde, night view
cathedrale.h1: Restaurant near Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde
cathedrale.hero_subtitle: Savour exceptional cuisine in the shadow of the age-old fortress
cathedrale.s1_title: Saint-Étienne Cathedral: sentinel of black basalt
cathedrale.s1_p1: Dominating the old town with its massive silhouette, Saint-Étienne Cathedral in Agde is a monument like no other. Built in the 12th century on the foundations of an older Carolingian structure, this cathedral-fortress was constructed entirely of black basalt stone, the volcanic material that gives Agde its architectural singularity. Its thick walls, battlements and keep-like bell tower attest to an era when religious buildings also had to protect the population from Barbary raids coming from the sea.
cathedrale.s1_p2: Listed as a historic monument since 1840, Saint-Étienne Cathedral is one of the oldest fortified buildings in Languedoc. Its single nave, austere and majestic, impresses with its soaring vault and the atmosphere of contemplation that reigns within. The side chapels house some remarkable works of sacred art, and the Romanesque apse, visible from Place de la Marine, recalls the time when Agde was an important river port.
cathedrale.s1_p3: The exterior of the building is no less fascinating. The powerful buttresses, arrow slits and parapet walk alone tell of centuries of turbulent history. Looking up towards the bell tower, you can make out the lighter stones of successive restorations, testimony to the constant will to preserve this jewel of Agde's heritage. The cathedral stands at the heart of a maze of narrow lanes and shaded squares that lead naturally to Place Jean Jaurès, the nerve centre of social life in the old town.
cathedrale.s2_title: Nine centuries of history within sight
cathedrale.s2_image_alt: Elegant interior of Le Divino restaurant near Agde Cathedral
cathedrale.s2_p1: Construction of the cathedral began around 1173, under the episcopate of Bishop Guillaume. From the outset the building was conceived as a fortress: Agde, a coastal town, was then regularly subjected to raids by Saracen pirates. The population had to be able to take refuge in the cathedral in the event of an attack, which explains the considerable thickness of its walls — nearly two metres in places — and the presence of defensive features unusual for a place of worship.
cathedrale.s2_p2: Over the following centuries, the cathedral was at once a place of prayer, a military refuge and a symbol of episcopal power over the town. The bishops of Agde sat here until the Revolution, and the building weathered the Wars of Religion, the conflicts with Béziers and the upheavals of Languedoc history without ever losing its sacred function.
cathedrale.s2_p3: Today, Saint-Étienne Cathedral is a must-see for any visitor to Agde. Guided tours organised by the Tourist Office reveal its secrets: the stonemasons' marks carved into the basalt, the crypt buried beneath the choir, and the panoramic view from the top of the keep-tower over the town's rooftops and the course of the Hérault.
cathedrale.s3_title: Le Divino: your restaurant in the shadow of the cathedral
cathedrale.s3_image_alt: Terrace of Le Divino restaurant, Place Jean Jaurès, next to Agde Cathedral
cathedrale.s3_p1: As you leave Saint-Étienne Cathedral, just a few steps take you to Place Jean Jaurès and the terrace of Le Divino. This closeness makes the restaurant the natural address for extending a cultural visit into a moment of gastronomic pleasure. Many visitors in fact discover Le Divino after admiring the cathedral — and return for its cuisine as much as for its setting.
cathedrale.s3_p2: The chef at Le Divino offers traditional French cuisine with a Mediterranean accent, prepared from fresh, seasonal produce. The fish arrives each morning from Grau d'Agde, the fruit and vegetables come from the market gardeners of the Hérault plain, and the meats are selected from regional farmers. Every plate tells of the terroir, with the same honesty with which the basalt stones tell the town's history.
cathedrale.s3_p3: The interior of the restaurant is also worth the detour. The dining room, warm and intimate, combines exposed stone and contemporary décor in a tasteful balance. In the evening, the soft lighting and candles create an atmosphere particularly suited to romantic dinners — the perfect setting after a twilight wander through the age-old lanes of the old town.
cathedrale.s4_title: Your day in Agde: cathedral, gastronomy and heritage
cathedrale.s4_step1_text: Begin by discovering Saint-Étienne Cathedral. Allow about an hour to appreciate the architecture, the nave and the view from the bell tower.
cathedrale.s4_step2_text: Head to Place Jean Jaurès for lunch on the terrace. The lunchtime menu offers excellent value with market produce.
cathedrale.s4_step3_text: Stroll along the walk on the banks of the Hérault, then visit the Agathois Museum to complete your immersion in the history of Agde.
cathedrale.practical_address: 5 Place Jean Jaurès, 34300 Agde — a 1-minute walk from Saint-Étienne Cathedral
cathedrale.practical_hours: Tuesday to Saturday 12pm-2pm / 7pm-10pm — Sunday 12pm-3.30pm — Closed Monday
cathedrale.practical_advice: Book for lunch in high season; the terrace is very popular after cathedral visits
cathedrale.cta_title: Book your table near the cathedral
cathedrale.cta_text: After discovering Saint-Étienne Cathedral, treat yourself to a moment of gastronomy at Le Divino. Lunch on the terrace or an intimate dinner — we welcome you on Place Jean Jaurès.

musee.seo_title: Restaurant near the Agathois Museum | Le Divino — Traditional Cuisine
musee.seo_description: Le Divino, a restaurant a few minutes from the Agathois Museum in Agde. French cuisine, terrace on Place Jean Jaurès. Perfect after visiting the museum and the Ephebe.
musee.seo_keywords: restaurant Agathois museum, restaurant historic centre Agde, lunch after museum Agde
musee.og_title: Restaurant near the Agathois Museum | Le Divino
musee.og_description: Traditional French cuisine a few minutes from the Agathois Museum. Terrace, local produce, Place Jean Jaurès in Agde.
musee.og_image_alt: Le Divino restaurant near the Agathois Museum in Agde
musee.hero_alt: Le Divino restaurant near the Agathois Museum, historic centre of Agde
musee.hero_subtitle: From the Ephebe of Agde to Le Divino's table: a journey between culture and gastronomy
musee.s1_title: The Agathois Museum: living memory of an age-old town
musee.s1_p1: Nestled in the heart of Agde's historic centre, the Agathois Museum — officially named the Jules Baudou Agathois Museum — is one of those places where history comes to life. Housed in a former mansion on Rue de la Fraternité, a few hundred metres from Place Jean Jaurès, it brings together collections of remarkable richness that retrace more than twenty-six centuries of Agde history, from its Greek founding to the present day.
musee.s1_p2: The museum owes much of its fame to an exceptional treasure: the Ephebe of Agde. This Hellenistic bronze statue, discovered in 1964 in the bed of the Hérault by an amateur diver, has become the emblem of the town. About 1.40 metres tall, it depicts a young man in a pose of striking grace. Specialists date the work to the 2nd century BC and still debate its exact origin — Roman or Greek. Whatever the case, its beauty and exceptional state of preservation make it one of the masterpieces of French underwater archaeology.
musee.s1_p3: Beyond the Ephebe, the museum's rooms unfold a fascinating panorama of Agde civilisation. The archaeological collections gather Greco-Roman amphorae, ancient coins and fishing and navigation tools that recall the town's maritime past. The rooms devoted to ethnography present traditional costumes, the Languedoc water jousts — that spectacular tradition still enlivening the summer festivals — and the artisanal skills that shaped the town's identity.
musee.s2_image_alt: Warm atmosphere at Le Divino restaurant, steps from the Agathois Museum
musee.s2_p1: The museum tour is organised into several thematic sections. The space devoted to prehistory and Antiquity presents the oldest evidence of human occupation in the lower Hérault valley: knapped flint, pottery, and above all the vestiges of the Greek colony of Agatha Tyche, founded around 525 BC by seafarers from Phocaea, via Massalia (Marseille).
musee.s2_p2: The maritime section is particularly evocative. Boat models, navigation instruments, old nets and hooks illustrate the intimate relationship Agde has always maintained with the sea and the river. Here you discover how the Agde fishermen developed specific fishing techniques suited to local conditions, and how the port of Agde played an important commercial role in Mediterranean trade.
musee.s2_p3: Finally, the rooms devoted to popular traditions are a true journey through time. Reconstructed Agde interiors, a collection of headdresses and festival costumes, traditional musical instruments: every display case tells a part of daily life in old Agde. The nautical jousts, inscribed on the intangible cultural heritage, hold pride of place with their trophies, banners and period photographs.
musee.s3_title: From museum to table: Le Divino awaits you
musee.s3_image_alt: Le Divino's terrace on Place Jean Jaurès, a few minutes from the Agathois Museum
musee.s3_p1: After a visit to the Agathois Museum, there is nothing like a good meal to extend the pleasure of discovery. Le Divino is a few minutes' walk away, gently up through the lanes of the historic centre to Place Jean Jaurès. The route is itself a pleasant stroll, letting you soak up the unique atmosphere of the basalt old town.
musee.s3_p2: Le Divino's cuisine maintains, in its own way, a strong link with Agde heritage. The chef draws inspiration from the culinary traditions of Languedoc and the Mediterranean — those very traditions the museum documents and preserves. Fish arriving that same morning from the port of Grau d'Agde, market vegetables, aromatic herbs from the garrigue: every ingredient is a tribute to the local terroir. The wine list celebrates the Languedoc grape varieties — Picpoul, Grenache, Syrah — that thrive on the region's volcanic soils.
musee.s3_p3: On the terrace, facing the basalt façades of Place Jean Jaurès, lunch takes on the air of a Mediterranean feast. The southern light, the murmur of the fountain, the scent of dishes drifting from the kitchen: it is a complete sensory experience that naturally crowns a cultural morning at the museum. In the evening the atmosphere becomes more intimate, and the square bathed in golden light invites a dinner for two or among friends.
musee.s4_title: A cultural and gourmet route through the centre of Agde
musee.s4_p1: To enjoy a full day in Agde, we suggest an itinerary that combines the riches of heritage with the pleasures of the table. Begin with a visit to the Agathois Museum in the morning, taking time to admire the Ephebe and explore the collections at your own pace. Allow about an hour and a half for a thorough visit.
musee.s4_p2: On leaving the museum, head towards Place Jean Jaurès through the lanes of the old centre. Make a detour via <link>Saint-Étienne Cathedral</link>, a stone's throw from the museum, to admire its basalt fortress silhouette. Then settle in at Le Divino for a lunch that celebrates the flavours of the Languedoc terroir.
musee.s4_p3: In the afternoon, continue your exploration with the <link>walk along the banks of the Hérault</link>. The quays offer a superb perspective over the old town and its dark stone façades. It is the ideal complement to a day blending historical discovery, gastronomic pleasure and a leisurely stroll by the water.
musee.practical_musee_distance: A 5-minute walk from Le Divino restaurant
musee.practical_musee_hours: Check opening hours with the Tourist Office
musee.cta_title: Book your table after the museum
musee.cta_text: From the Ephebe of Agde to the flavours of Languedoc, extend your cultural journey with an exceptional meal at Le Divino. Sunny terrace or intimate dining room — we await you on Place Jean Jaurès.

chateau.seo_title: Restaurant near Château Laurens in Agde | Le Divino — Place Jean Jaurès
chateau.seo_description: Le Divino, a restaurant a few minutes from Château Laurens in Agde. Traditional French cuisine, terrace on Place Jean Jaurès. Lunch or dinner after visiting the Belle Époque villa.
chateau.seo_keywords: restaurant Château Laurens Agde, dining near Château Laurens, restaurant Belle Époque villa Agde, where to dine after Château Laurens
chateau.og_title: Restaurant near Château Laurens in Agde | Le Divino
chateau.og_description: Traditional French cuisine a few minutes from Château Laurens, Agde's Belle Époque jewel. Terrace, local produce, Place Jean Jaurès.
chateau.og_image_alt: Le Divino restaurant near Château Laurens in Agde
chateau.hero_alt: Le Divino restaurant near Château Laurens in Agde, night view
chateau.h1: Restaurant near Château Laurens in Agde
chateau.hero_subtitle: Extend your visit to the Belle Époque villa with an exceptional table
chateau.s1_title: Château Laurens: an oriental dream on the banks of the Hérault
chateau.s1_p1: Set in the heart of the Île des Loisirs, on the peaceful banks of the Hérault, Château Laurens is arguably the most surprising monument in Agde. Far from the austere basalt fortresses that fill the old town, this villa tells another story: that of a man of the turn of the 20th century, a wealthy heir and great traveller, who wished to make his home an architectural manifesto. Built between 1898 and 1901 for Emmanuel Laurens, the château blends, with rare boldness, Art Nouveau, orientalism, the neo-Egyptian style and Greco-Roman mythology. The result is a unique eclectic residence, long forgotten and then triumphantly restored.
chateau.s1_p2: After several decades of silence and a long restoration campaign led by the Agde Hérault Méditerranée agglomeration community, Château Laurens reopened to the public in 2023. Word of mouth did the rest: within a few months the villa became one of the must-see visits in Languedoc, drawing art lovers and the curious alike, who discover, astonished, painted ceilings, orientalist stained glass and mosaic floors of rare delicacy.
chateau.s1_p3: The visit passes through a succession of themed salons: the Lotus room with its Egyptian columns, the oriental boudoir with carved mashrabiyas, the library with its floral panelling, or the music room with its original organ. Each room is a stage, each detail a nod to Emmanuel Laurens's travels in Egypt, India and Greece. You leave the visit with the feeling of having passed through the dream of a wealthy aesthete, frozen at the dawn of the 20th century.
chateau.s2_title: From an heir's dream to a public jewel
chateau.s2_image_alt: Elegant dining room at Le Divino, a table reserved after visiting Château Laurens
chateau.s2_p1: Emmanuel Laurens, son of an Agde family enriched by trade and pharmacy, had inherited a considerable fortune while young, following the unexpected death of an Egyptian relative. Rather than invest it, he chose to spend it giving shape to his passions: music, travel and a certain idea of beauty. He called upon the architect Olivier Garros, then a host of craftsmen — painters, mosaicists, ironworkers, cabinetmakers — to fashion this villa that was to be, in his words, "the fulfilled dream of a free man".
chateau.s2_p2: After his death in 1959, the château passed from hand to hand, was for a time a holiday residence and then abandoned. The decades of neglect nearly destroyed its soul: murals threatened by damp, cracked stained glass, mosaics vanishing under deposits. It took nearly twenty years of study and works to restore the monument's original splendour. Listed as a Historic Monument, Château Laurens is today an international example of heritage restoration.
chateau.s2_p3: The park surrounding the villa is also worth the detour. Century-old trees, wisteria, a channel of running water: everything invites you to extend the visit outdoors, in a peaceful atmosphere that contrasts pleasantly with the exuberant richness of the interiors. In spring and summer, cultural events — concerts, readings, temporary exhibitions — regularly enliven the site.
chateau.s3_title: Le Divino: the table that extends the dream
chateau.s3_image_alt: Terrace of Le Divino restaurant, Place Jean Jaurès, ideal after visiting Château Laurens
chateau.s3_p1: After a visit as rich as that of Château Laurens, there is a strong urge to sit down, breathe and let yourself be carried by a good meal. Place Jean Jaurès, where Le Divino is located, is about ten minutes' walk from the château along the banks of the Hérault — a pleasant stroll that naturally extends the contemplative mood of the visit.
chateau.s3_p2: Our traditional French cuisine, with a Mediterranean accent, gives pride of place to fresh produce and local producers. The fish is caught at Grau d'Agde, the vegetables come from the market gardeners of the plain, the wines are selected from the finest Languedoc estates. It is this same concern for authenticity and transmission that inspires both Le Divino's cuisine and the restoration of Château Laurens: keeping heritage alive without freezing it.
chateau.s3_p3: The restaurant's setting, warm and refined, extends this atmosphere. The shaded terrace is ideal for lunch after the visit; the intimate indoor dining room welcomes evening dinners in soft lighting. Many château visitors, French and foreign alike, end their day with us — and tell us, over a glass, about the day's discoveries.
chateau.s4_title: A day in Agde: Château Laurens, gastronomy and heritage
chateau.s4_step1_text: Allow about 1h30 to explore the salons and the park and fully enjoy the restored décor. Booking recommended, especially in high season.
chateau.s4_step2_text: Walk to Place Jean Jaurès along the Hérault. Our lunchtime menu, based on the day's market, offers a fine contrast with the richness of the château.
chateau.s4_step3_text: Stroll through the lanes of the old town, discover the black basalt Saint-Étienne Cathedral, then return along the Hérault at sunset.
chateau.practical_address: 5 Place Jean Jaurès, 34300 Agde — about a 10-minute walk from Château Laurens along the Hérault
chateau.practical_hours: Tuesday to Saturday 12pm-2pm / 7pm-10pm — Sunday 12pm-3.30pm — Closed Monday
chateau.practical_advice: Book your table in advance on the château's busy days (Wednesday and Saturday afternoon in particular)
chateau.cta_title: Book your table after Château Laurens
chateau.cta_text: After admiring the oriental salons and stained glass of Château Laurens, treat yourself to a lunch or dinner worthy of your day. We welcome you on Place Jean Jaurès, a few minutes from the château.
